Description.Text: AWS Cost & Usage Report (CUR) usage data at HOURLY granularity (usage_date is the hourly usage-start timestamp), WITHOUT resource_id. Use for intra-day / hourly cost & usage patterns, peak-hour analysis, and diurnal trends. Rolling ~30-day window. IMPORTANT: contains only usage line items (Usage, DiscountedUsage, SavingsPlanCoveredUsage) - no credits, refunds, fees, or tax. For resource-level detail use resource_view; for longer history use summary_view.

InlineCustomInstruction.InstructionText:
FIELD NAMES
All field names in these instructions are the dataset (QuickSight/SPICE) field names - reference them exactly as written. In the CUR COLUMN MAPPING below, the format is `dataset_field -> source CUR column`: the right side is the original AWS Cost and Usage Report column, shown so you can apply standard CUR knowledge. Query using the dataset_field (left side), not the CUR column.

WHEN TO USE
Use hourly_view for hourly/intra-day analysis: usage-by-hour, peak vs off-peak, diurnal patterns. Window is a rolling ~30 days; usage_date is the HOURLY usage-start timestamp (not truncated to day).

IMPORTANT SCOPE LIMIT
This view contains ONLY usage-type line items: 'Usage', 'DiscountedUsage', 'SavingsPlanCoveredUsage'. It has NO credits, refunds, fees, tax, or RI/SP fee rows. Do NOT use it for total-bill, discount, or credit analysis - use summary_view for those. There is no resource_id - use resource_view for per-resource questions.

COST COLUMNS
- unblended_cost: raw line-item cost (default metric).
- reservation_effective_cost / savings_plan_effective_cost: effective cost for RI- and SP-covered usage. Combine with unblended_cost for an effective-cost view of on-demand usage.
- No public_cost or amortized_cost column here.

CUR COLUMN MAPPING
usage_date -> line_item_usage_start_date (HOURLY)
billing_period -> bill_billing_period_start_date
payer_account_id -> bill_payer_account_id
linked_account_id -> line_item_usage_account_id
product_code -> line_item_product_code
service -> product_servicecode (friendly name)
operation -> line_item_operation
charge_type -> line_item_line_item_type (usage types only, see scope limit)
usage_type -> line_item_usage_type
item_description -> line_item_line_item_description
pricing_unit -> pricing_unit
pricing_term -> pricing_term
region -> product_region
reservation_a_r_n -> reservation_reservation_a_r_n
savings_plan_a_r_n -> savings_plan_savings_plan_a_r_n

Cost sources: unblended_cost -> line_item_unblended_cost; usage_quantity -> line_item_usage_amount; reservation_effective_cost -> reservation_effective_cost; savings_plan_effective_cost -> savings_plan_savings_plan_effective_cost.

USAGE & ACCOUNTS
usage_quantity: consumed quantity in pricing_unit.
account_id / account_name: friendly account identifiers (may be NULL if mapping not configured).

ORGANIZATIONAL TAXONOMY (may be present)
Depending on the CUR and this deployment's configuration, additional columns for organizational taxonomy may be present - user-defined cost allocation tags (typically prefixed resource_tags_ / user_) and AWS Cost Categories (typically prefixed cost_category_). When present, use them to group cost by organizational dimensions such as team, environment, cost center, project, or business unit (chargeback/showback). Inspect available columns at query time; do not assume a fixed set.

QUERY TIPS
- Hour-of-day profile: GROUP BY hour(usage_date).
- Peak detection: ORDER BY SUM(usage_quantity) or SUM(unblended_cost) per hour.
- Service/account slice: filter service / linked_account_id, then GROUP BY usage_date.
